# B08: Stakeholder Intelligence

## Nico — Left Lane Capital
**Role:** Contact at Left Lane VC  
**Disposition:** Responsive to Careerspan pitch  
**Key Interests:**
- Access to top quality young engineering talent
- Quick scanning against candidate pools
- Silent vs. explicit criteria (covert scanning) — "oh shit" moment
- Reducing 10-15% recruiter surcharges for portfolio founders
- Potentially India-based engineering at scale
**Decision Power:** Will take analysis to senior partner; not the final decision maker  
**Next Step:** Receives V's analysis package tomorrow morning  
**Risk:** Senior partner may not see enough value without a live placement proof point  

## Left Lane Senior Partner (unnamed)
**Role:** Decision maker at Left Lane  
**Disposition:** Unknown — depends on Nico's presentation of the analysis  
**Key Interests:** Likely has "pet companies" he wants to help specifically  
**Decision Power:** Final say on whether Left Lane engages  
**Next Step:** Nico presents analysis; if positive, 4-person meeting (V, Shivam, Nico, Partner)  

## Priya — Cobalt ID
**Role:** Founder, Cobalt ID  
**Disposition:** Desperate ("at this point, we'll take anyone"); survived 6 H1B cycles  
**Profile:** Older (HBS), Indian founder, hiring engineers in NYC  
**Key Interests:** Execution speed, reducing interview burden, engineering talent (domestic or India)  
**Decision Power:** Likely sole decision maker or co-founder with authority  
**Next Step:** V setting up 3-way call  
**Opportunity:** Multiple engineering positions = team deal potential  

## Ivar — Morpheus Group
**Role:** Associated with Andrew's Shortlist business  
**Disposition:** V meeting him next week  
**Key Interests:** Unknown — V needs to sound out appetite for Careerspan tech  
**Risk:** Could piss Ivar off if perceived as ripping off Shortlist concept  
**Opportunity:** Careerspan branded on Shortlist would be a major distribution win  

## Andrew — Shortlist / Morpheus Group
**Role:** Runs Shortlist  
**Disposition:** Practical, focused, not venture-backed empire-building type  
**Key Interests:** Cultivating specific brand, not building internal tech  
**Decision Power:** Owns the Shortlist business  
**Opportunity:** Has both employers and operational execution — "best of both worlds"  
**Note:** Won't only hire Americans — India placement opportunities remain  

## Brex Contacts
**Role:** Financial services for startups  
**Disposition:** Unknown for talent pipeline pitch  
**Context:** Shivam's relationship is US-India focused; talent pipeline is a new angle  
**Potential:** Every India startup selling into US will have a Delaware C Corp → Brex customer  
**Next Step:** Shivam talking to them Feb 19  

## Manifest
**Role:** O-1 visa services  
**Disposition:** "Super aggressive" according to Shivam  
**Key Context:** Shivam referring them business for free currently; wants to monetize  
**Revenue Model:** Unclear — may be success-fee or deposit-based (Shivam to clarify)  
**Opportunity:** Could fund campus distribution for Careerspan as a marketing/awareness play  
**Risk:** If purely success-fee based, campus funnel may not align with their economics  

## Docsum
**Role:** Previous employer prospect  
**Disposition:** Unresponsive despite saying they liked the process  
**Context:** Had two offers negged at last minute; spending massive interview hours  
**Mystery:** Why haven't they engaged further despite obvious pain?  
**Risk:** Could indicate Careerspan's value prop isn't landing with employers  

## Emory Contact
**Role:** University alumni office  
**Context:** Was going to launch Careerspan to Wise Hearts program ($1K+/year donors for 5+ years)  
**Status:** Checked email today — V following up
